source.book_title: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
source.note: Recipes from the southern parts of Kerala, particularly Trivandrum.

recipes.title: Ada Dosa
recipes.subtitle: A crisp, savoury breakfast pancake made from a medley of rice and grams
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash and soak raw rice and all the grams (black gram, split pigeon peas, split green gram and split bengal gram) in plenty of water for 3-4 hours.
Grind the red chillies first, then add the soaked mixture in small quantities to make a coarse batter; keep aside.
Grind onion, green chillies and curry leaves together to make a coarse paste.
Add this paste to the batter along with asafetida and salt; add water to get the desired consistency.
Heat the dosa pan and grease lightly with oil.
Pour a big spoonful of batter in the centre of the pan and spread with the back of the spoon to make a thin pancake.
When the dosa appears cooked on top, turn it over with a spatula.
Remove when crisp and serve hot with chutney.

recipes.title: Oratti
recipes.subtitle: A thick wheat and coconut pancake, traditionally served with a sunny-side-up egg
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with fried egg (sunny side up / egg bulls-eye).

steps.instruction: Mix wheat flour, grated coconut and salt; add water gradually to make a very soft dough (consistency between chapatti dough and dosa batter).
steps.tip: The dough should be softer than chapatti dough — this is what gives oratti its tender texture.

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a pan and transfer a small amount of the dough to the pan; spread the dough with your fingers to make a thick pancake.
Turn over a few times until the pancake is well cooked on both sides.
Serve hot with a fried egg (sunny side up).

recipes.title: Dosa
recipes.subtitle: The classic fermented rice and black gram pancake — crisp at the edges, soft at the centre
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve hot with sambar and chutney.

steps.instruction: Wash and soak rice and black gram separately in plenty of water for 7 - 8 hours; add fenugreek to the black gram while soaking.
Grind the rice and black gram separately to a fine batter adding water; mix the ground rice and black gram and add salt; cover and keep aside to ferment overnight.
Heat the dosa pan and grease lightly with oil.
Pour a big spoonful of batter in the centre of the pan and spread with the back of the spoon to make a thin pancake.
When the dosa appears cooked on top, turn it over with a spatula.
Remove when crisp and serve hot with sambar and chutney.

recipes.title: Gothambu Dosa
recipes.subtitle: An instant wheat-flour pancake — no soaking, no fermenting, ready in minutes
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Mix wheat flour and salt; add water to make a fine batter.
Heat the dosa pan and grease lightly with oil.
Pour a big spoonful of batter in the centre of the pan and spread with the back of the spoon to make a thin pancake.
When the dosa appears cooked on top, turn it over with a spatula.
Remove when crisp and serve hot with chutney.

recipes.title: Kaduku Varatha Appam
recipes.subtitle: A rice pancake tempered with mustard seeds, coconut and curry leaves
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash and soak the raw rice and boiled rice in plenty of water for 4 - 5 hours.
Grind the rice mixture to a coarse batter.
Grind the grated coconut, dry red chillies and curry leaves separately to a coarse paste; add this to the batter with salt and water to get the desired consistency.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, remove and mix it with the batter.
Heat the dosa pan and grease lightly with oil.
Pour a big spoonful of batter in the centre of the pan and spread with the back of the spoon to make a thin pancake; spread a little oil over the dosa.
When the dosa appears cooked on top, turn it over with a spatula.
Remove when crisp and serve hot with chutney.

recipes.title: Idli
recipes.subtitle: Feather-light steamed rice and black gram cakes — the gentle breakfast staple
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve hot with sambar and chutney.

steps.instruction: Wash and soak rice and black gram separately in plenty of water for 7 - 8 hours; add fenugreek to the black gram while soaking.
Grind the rice and black gram separately to a fine batter adding water; mix the ground rice and black gram and add salt; cover and keep aside to ferment overnight.
Heat water in a steamer; pour the batter onto idli trays and steam cook for 5 - 10 minutes.
steps.tip: You can use a skewer to see if the idlis are cooked.

steps.instruction: Serve hot with sambar and chutney.

recipes.title: Appam
recipes.subtitle: Lacy-edged, bowl-shaped rice hoppers with a pillowy centre — Kerala's most beloved breakfast
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with chicken or potato stew.

steps.instruction: Prepare yeast solution by mixing sugar and yeast with half a cup of warm water; keep aside for 30 minutes until a froth appears (ferments).

Grind raw rice, cooked rice and grated coconut with water to make a fine batter; add yeast solution and grind further for a few more minutes; transfer to a bowl; add salt and keep aside to ferment for about 4 - 5 hours.
Add 3 tbsp of sugar to the fermented batter; add more water to the batter if necessary.
Heat the 'Appam chatti' (grease lightly with oil if the pan is not non-stick).

Pour 3 - 4 tbsp of the batter into the 'chatti'; swirl the 'chatti' by hand so that about 2 inches of the sides of the chatti is thinly coated and the remaining batter collects at the centre; cover with a tight fitting lid and cook on a low flame for about 3 minutes.
When it is ready, the edges resemble crisp lace and the centre is soft and well risen.
Remove from the chatti with a spatula and serve with chicken or potato stew.

recipes.title: Idiappam
recipes.subtitle: Delicate rice-flour string hoppers steamed in coconut — soft, light, and made to soak up curry
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with stew or any curry of your choice.

steps.instruction: Mix raw rice flour with salt; add boiling water gradually and knead into a soft dough.
Boil water in a steamer; grease the concave depressions of the 'idiappam thattu' with oil and fill it with the prepared dough using a 'seva nazhi' (idiappam presser/sieve) in a circular motion.

Add 2 tsp of grated coconut over this and then press more of the dough over it to sandwich the grated coconut; repeat for all tiers of the tray; place the trays into the steamer and cook the idiappams for about 5 minutes.
Serve the idiappams with stew or any curry of your choice.

recipes.title: Puttu
recipes.subtitle: Cylinders of steamed rice flour and coconut — a Kerala breakfast that holds together just so
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with Kadala Curry and Pappadom, or with banana and sugar. Replace rice flour with wheat flour to make 'gothambu' (wheat) puttu.

steps.instruction: Mix rice flour and salt thoroughly in a large bowl; moisten the flour by sprinkling with water gradually and mixing continuously with your hand; the flour should be just moist enough to hold together when pressed between your fingers.

Add 1 tbsp of grated coconut to the perforated bottom lid of the 'Puttukutti'; add a thick layer of the rice flour mixture over it (about 2 - 3 inches) followed by another thin layer of grated coconut; continue for 3 to 4 layers and end with grated coconut on top.
Boil water in the steamer and place the puttukutti on top; cover the puttukutti with its lid; remove the cooked puttu when a steady strong steam comes off the lid.
Serve with Kadala Curry and Pappadom or with banana and sugar.

recipes.title: Kozhukatta
recipes.subtitle: Soft steamed rice dumplings spiced with cumin and sweetened with coconut
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve hot with curry of your choice.

steps.instruction: Wash and soak rice in plenty of water for 4 - 5 hours.
Grind the soaked rice in small quantities to make a soft dough.
Grind cumin and grated coconut to a coarse paste; add this to the dough with salt and mix well.
Make small balls of this dough.
These balls may be either cooked in boiling water or steam cooked; when cooked, they will be firm in consistency.
Serve hot with curry of your choice.

recipes.title: Puri
recipes.subtitle: Puffed, golden wheat discs fried to order — simple and celebratory at once
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with bhaji, chana masala or any curry of your choice.

ingredients.name: Oil
ingredients.preparation: to fry and roll out the dough

steps.instruction: In a large bowl, combine the wheat flour and refined wheat flour; add 1 tbsp of oil to the mixture and make a pliable soft dough gradually adding water; cover the dough with a wet towel and keep aside for 1 - 2 hours.
Before rolling out, knead the dough again and make lime-sized balls of it; roll the balls into 5-inch discs on a board.
Heat oil in a wok or kadai; when the oil is hot, add the puris one by one and fry, turning once; remove and drain on paper napkins.
Serve with bhaji, chana masala or any curry of your choice.

recipes.title: Uppuma
recipes.subtitle: A warm, savoury breakfast of semolina toasted with ginger, onion and coconut
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve hot with bananas or any side dish of your choice.

steps.instruction: Heat oil/ghee in a pan; add onions, green chillies and ginger; sauté until the onion becomes tender.
Add 4 cups of water and salt and bring to a boil; add grated coconut.
Add rava to the boiling water little by little stirring continuously so that no lumps are formed; cover the pan and cook on low heat until uppuma is soft and lump-free.
steps.tip: If using oil initially, add a little ghee at the end to enhance the flavors.

steps.instruction: Serve hot with bananas or any side dish of your choice.

recipes.title: Green Chutney
recipes.subtitle: A fresh coconut chutney with green chilli and ginger — the quiet companion to dosa and idli
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Grind grated coconut, green chillies, ginger, shallots, tamarind juice and salt to a fine paste; add water to your required consistency.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; transfer the seasoning to the chutney.
Serve with dosa or idli.

recipes.title: Red Chutney
recipes.subtitle: A warming coconut chutney stained red with dry red chillies and finished with curry leaves
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Grind grated coconut, dry red chillies, green chilli, ginger, shallots, tamarind juice and salt to a fine paste; add water to your required consistency.

Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; transfer the chutney to the pan and heat on a low flame for about 3 minutes.
Serve with dosa or idli.

recipes.title: Rasam
recipes.subtitle: A thin, fragrant, tamarind-and-pepper broth — the everyday comfort soup of a Kerala home
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Cook the split pigeon peas and tomato in a pressure cooker with 2 cups of water.
Grind red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin seeds, garlic, turmeric powder and black pepper powder with a little water to a fine paste.
Transfer the cooked tomato and the peas (sauce only) by passing through a sieve into a saucepan. Add the ground masala, salt and asafetida. Bring to a boil and remove from heat.
steps.tip: Passing through a sieve gives rasam its characteristic thin, clear body.

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds. When they pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute.
Add this seasoning to the rasam and mix well.
Serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Sambar
recipes.subtitle: The classic mixed-vegetable lentil stew that turns everyday rice into a meal
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Pairs with rice, dosa, idli, or appam.

steps.instruction: Cook the split pigeon peas in a pressure cooker with 2 cups of water.
Remove the cooker lid and add the cut vegetables, turmeric powder, salt and asafetida with 2 cups of water; cook on a high flame.
When the vegetables are cooked, add coriander powder, tamarind juice, okra and tomato and bring to a boil.
Add fenugreek powder and coriander leaves, mix well and remove from heat.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds. When they pop, add dry red chillies, curry leaves and thinly sliced shallots; sauté for a minute.
Add this seasoning to the sambar and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Rasavada
recipes.subtitle: Lentil fritters floated in hot rasam — soft, spiced, soaked through
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with rice. The rasam is made separately — see the Rasam recipe.

steps.instruction: Prepare the Rasam separately (see Rasam recipe).
Wash and soak the split pigeon peas for 1 hour; drain and keep aside.
Grind the peas to a coarse mixture.
To the ground peas add onions, green chillies, red chilli powder, asafetida, salt and mix well.
Divide the mixture into equal lime-sized balls and pat gently into a small patty; deep-fry the vadas in batches and drain on paper napkins.
Add the fried vadas to the Rasam.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Pulinkarry
recipes.subtitle: A tangy, tamarind-forward lentil and spice curry tempered with mustard and shallots
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: In a pan roast the coriander seeds, dry red chillies, split pigeon peas, black pepper, asafetida and shallots until the raw smell disappears.
Grind the roasted masala to a fine powder.
Transfer the ground masala to a saucepan; add 3 cups of water, tamarind juice, turmeric powder and salt and bring to a boil and remove from fire.

Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies, curry leaves and thinly sliced shallots and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the curry and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Koottu Curry
recipes.subtitle: A coconut-milk potato curry fragrant with whole spices — gentle enough for breakfast, rich enough for a feast
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with rice, dosa or idli.

steps.instruction: In a pan lightly roast coriander seeds, dry red chillies, fennel seeds, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon and star anise.
Grind the roasted masala to a fine powder.

Add the ground masala, onions and green chillies to the potatoes in a saucepan and cook with 3 cups of water; when the potatoes are cooked and the gravy is reduced to the required consistency add the coconut milk and bring to a boil and remove from fire.

Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies, curry leaves and thinly sliced shallots and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the curry and mix well.
Serve with rice, dosa or idli.

recipes.title: Vada Koottu Curry
recipes.subtitle: Koottu Curry enriched with freshly fried black gram vadas
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with rice. The koottu curry is prepared separately — see the Koottu Curry recipe.

ingredients.name: Koottu Curry
ingredients.preparation: prepared separately — see Koottu Curry recipe

steps.instruction: Prepare the Koottu Curry separately (see Koottu Curry recipe).
Wash and soak the black gram in plenty of water for 6 hours; drain and keep aside.
Grind the black gram with a little water to make a fluffy batter; transfer the batter into a bowl, add salt and mix well.

Heat oil in a deep wok (kadai); with a wet palm take a ball sized batter and drop into the hot oil; repeat based on the size of your pan; deep-fry until they turn crisp and golden brown; remove and drain the vadas on paper napkins.
Add the fried vada to the curry.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Sheema Chakka Koottu Curry
recipes.subtitle: Breadfruit simmered in roasted whole-spice masala and coconut milk
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with rice. Sheema chakka is breadfruit (bakri-chajhar).

steps.instruction: In a pan lightly roast coriander seeds, dry red chillies, fennel seeds, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon and star anise.
Grind the roasted masala to a fine powder.

Add the ground masala, salt, onions and green chillies to the breadfruit in a saucepan and cook with 3 cups of water; when the breadfruit is cooked and the gravy is reduced to the required consistency add the coconut milk and bring to a boil and remove from fire.

Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies, curry leaves and thinly sliced shallots and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the curry and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Mixed Vegetable Koottu Curry
recipes.subtitle: A wholesome split-gram and vegetable medley tempered with browned coconut
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash and cook the split bengal gram in 2 cups of water; when the bengal gram is well cooked add the cut vegetables, turmeric powder and salt and cook on a medium heat; add more water if required.
Heat 1 tsp oil in a pan, add 3 cups of grated coconut, garlic, pepper corns, curry leaves and roast well; add red chilli powder and roast for a minute.
Grind the roasted masala to a coarse powder.
Transfer the ground masala to the cooked vegetables and bring to a boil and remove from fire.
Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds, dry red chilli and 1/2 cup grated coconut and roast until the coconut turns brown; add this mixture and a few curry leaves to the curry and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Vellarikka and Manga Ozhichukuttan
recipes.subtitle: Cucumber and raw mango stewed with coconut and yoghurt — cool, tangy, everyday Kerala
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Remove the skin of the cucumber and mango and cut into small pieces.
Grind grated coconut, cumin seeds and green chillies to a fine paste; add yoghurt and blend with the ground masala.

In a saucepan, cook the cucumber with red chilli powder, turmeric powder, salt and 2 cups of water; when the cucumber is cooked, add the raw mangoes and cook for about 3 minutes; add the yoghurt masala blend to the cooked vegetables, simmer for a few minutes and remove from fire.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies, curry leaves and fenugreek seeds and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the curry and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Pazham Manga Ozhichukuttan
recipes.subtitle: Whole ripe mangoes simmered in a yoghurt-coconut gravy — sweet, sour and warming all at once
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with rice. You can substitute the ripe mango with pineapple.

steps.instruction: Grind grated coconut, cumin seeds and green chillies to a fine paste; add yoghurt and blend with the ground masala.

Remove the skin of the ripe mangoes and cook the whole mango with red chilli powder, turmeric powder, salt and a little water; add the yoghurt masala blend to the cooked mangoes and simmer for a few minutes; add fenugreek powder and remove from fire.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the curry and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Thenga Aracha Ozhichukuttan
recipes.subtitle: A gently spiced coconut gravy finished with tamarind and fenugreek
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Grind grated coconut, dry red chillies and turmeric powder to a very fine paste; add the cumin seeds and shallots and grind further.
Transfer the ground masala to a saucepan; add fenugreek powder, tamarind juice, salt and 1 1/2 cups of water and bring to a boil and remove from fire.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the curry and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Pacha Theeyal
recipes.subtitle: An unroasted-coconut theeyal — fresh, light, and green in character
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Cut all the vegetables into small pieces.
Grind grated coconut, dry red chillies, cumin and shallots to a fine paste.
In a saucepan cook the vegetables with turmeric powder, salt and 1 cup of water; when the vegetables are cooked add tamarind juice and ground masala; bring to a boil and remove from fire.

Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies, curry leaves and thinly sliced shallots and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the curry and mix well.

recipes.title: Varutharacha Theeyal
recipes.subtitle: The classic roasted-coconut theeyal — dark, nutty, and deeply savoury
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

ingredients.name: Shallots
ingredients.preparation: cut into 2 pieces each, 4 thinly sliced

steps.instruction: Cut the yam and drumstick into small pieces.
In a pan roast grated coconut, coriander seeds, 4 thinly sliced shallots and 4 dry red chillies.
Grind the roasted masala with a little water to a fine paste.

In a saucepan cook the yam with 1 cup of water; when the yam is 3/4 cooked, add drumstick, cut shallots, green chillies, turmeric and salt; when the vegetables are well cooked, add tamarind juice and the ground masala and bring to a boil (add more water if required); remove from fire.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add 2 dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the cooked vegetables and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Ulli Theeyal
recipes.subtitle: Shallots browned in a roasted-coconut gravy — rich, dark and unmistakably Kerala
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Slice shallots into small pieces and keep aside.
In a pan roast the coconut, coriander seeds, dry red chillies and 4 shallots till they are well browned.
Grind the roasted masala with a little water to a fine paste.

Heat oil in saucepan and add mustard seeds, dry red chillies, curry leaves and sauté; add the sliced shallots and green chillies to this mixture and brown lightly; add the ground masala, tamarind juice, fenugreek powder, salt and 1 cup of water and bring to a boil; remove from fire.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Kadala Curry
recipes.subtitle: Brown chickpeas in a dark roasted-spice gravy finished with coconut milk — the classic puttu companion
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Soak the chickpeas in plenty of water for 5 - 6 hours.
Cook the chickpeas with salt and 4 cups of water in a pressure cooker and keep aside.
Sauté onion and green chillies and keep aside.
In a pan roast coriander seeds, dry red chillies, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon and fennel seeds.
Grind the roasted masala to a fine powder; add this roasted masala powder to 1/4 cup cooked chickpeas and a little water and grind to a fine paste.

In a saucepan with the cooked chickpeas add the sautéed onion and green chillies and the ground masala; add water and salt if necessary and bring to a boil; add coconut milk, mix well and bring to a boil again.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seed; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies, green chillies and curry leaves and sauté well; add this seasoning to the kadala curry.
Serve with puttu or appam.

recipes.title: Parippu
recipes.subtitle: A simple green-gram dal with coconut and cumin — quiet, nourishing, always welcome
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash and cook the split green gram with 2 cups of water; add salt and keep aside.
Grind the grated coconut, cumin seeds, turmeric powder and garlic with a little water to a fine paste.
In a saucepan add the ground masala to the cooked dal (add more water if necessary) and bring to a boil and remove from fire; add coconut oil and curry leaves and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Palak Parippu Curry
recipes.subtitle: Green gram dal folded with chopped spinach and a coconut-cumin paste
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash and cook the split green gram with 2 cups of water; add the chopped spinach and salt and keep aside.
Grind the grated coconut, cumin seeds, turmeric powder and garlic with a little water to a fine paste.
In a saucepan add the ground masala to the cooked dal and spinach mixture (add more water if necessary) and bring to a boil and remove from fire.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the curry and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Pulisserry
recipes.subtitle: A gently soured coconut-yoghurt curry finished with mustard and fenugreek
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Grind grated coconut, cumin seeds, green chillies and turmeric powder with a little water to a fine paste; add yoghurt to ground masala paste, blend and keep aside.

Heat oil in a saucepan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies, fenugreek seeds and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add the yoghurt masala blend and salt and cook over a low flame; remove from fire as soon as the pulisserry starts to simmer.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Moru Curry
recipes.subtitle: A soothing buttermilk curry spiced with ginger and chillies
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a saucepan and add mustard seeds, dry red chillies and curry leaves, shallots, garlic, ginger and green chillies and sauté well.

When the mix turns golden brown, add turmeric powder, cumin powder, fenugreek powder, chilli powder, mix well and heat on a low flame for 1 minute; add buttermilk with 1/2 a cup of water and salt; heat over a low flame stirring continuously; remove from heat when the moru curry starts to simmer.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Erissery
recipes.subtitle: Red beans, raw plantain, yam and taro bound by a coconut-pepper paste and crowned with browned coconut
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with rice. You may use 1 medium sized yellow pumpkin instead of raw plantain, yam & taro. Raw plantain is known as ethakka.

steps.instruction: Wash and cook the red beans in water and keep aside.
Cut the raw plantains, yam and taro into bite-sized pieces; soak the cut plantains in water with 1/2 a tsp of turmeric powder for 5 minutes to remove its stain and keep aside.
Grind 3/4 cup grated coconut, red chilli powder, pepper powder, cumin seeds and garlic with a little water to a coarse paste.

In a saucepan with the cooked red beans add the cut vegetables with salt and turmeric powder; add more water if required and cook over medium heat until the vegetables are well cooked; add the ground masala and bring to a boil; remove from heat and keep aside.

Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustards seeds pop, add black gram, dry red chillies, curry leaves and 1/4 cup of grated coconut and roast until the grated coconut turns brown; add this mixture to the erissery and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Kalan
recipes.subtitle: Yam stewed to thickness in a peppery coconut-yoghurt gravy — the festive Kerala pickle-curry
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Remove skin, clean and cut the yam into small pieces.
Grind grated coconut, 2 green chillies and cumin seeds with a little water to a fine paste; add yoghurt to ground masala paste, blend and keep aside.

In a saucepan with the cut yam, add 6 green chillies, red chilli powder, black pepper powder, turmeric powder and salt and cook with 1 cup of water until all water evaporates; add the yoghurt masala blend and salt to the cooked yam and gradually bring to a boil, stirring the mixture continuously; continue boiling the mixture, stirring occasionally until the kalan acquires a thick consistency; add fenugreek powder before removing from heat.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the curry and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Olan
recipes.subtitle: A pale, serene curry of ash gourd and black-eyed beans in coconut milk — Onam sadhya essential
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash and cook the black eyed beans in 2 cups of water; once the beans are cooked, drain excess water; add fresh water to the cooked beans to wash the colour of the beans away and keep aside.
Cut the ash gourd and taro into small cubes.

In a saucepan with the cooked beans add ash gourd, taro, green chillies and salt with 1/4 cup of water; cook on medium heat until the vegetables are well cooked and most of the water has evaporated; add thick coconut milk and bring to a boil; add coconut oil and curry leaves and remove from heat.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Avial
recipes.subtitle: The quintessential mixed-vegetable curry bound by a coconut paste, tangy with tamarind and scented with curry leaves
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Cut all vegetables into 1 - 1.5 inch pieces.
Grind grated coconut, cumin seeds, green chillies and curry leaves into a coarse paste.

In a saucepan cook yam in water until 3/4 done; add the rest of the vegetables with turmeric powder, red chilli powder and salt; add water if necessary and cook on a medium flame; when the vegetables are well cooked and water has evaporated, add tamarind juice and then the ground masala; cook over a low flame for 2 minutes to reduce the gravy to a thick consistency; add coconut oil and a few curry leaves and remove from heat.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Kaya & Chembu Avial
recipes.subtitle: A simpler avial of just plantain and taro in a coconut-red chilli paste
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Cut the plantain and taro into small pieces; soak the cut plantains in water with turmeric to remove its stain.
Grind grated coconut, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, cumin seeds, garlic and curry leaves to a coarse paste.

In a saucepan with the cut vegetables add the ground masala and salt and 1/2 a cup of water; cook the vegetables until the gravy is reduced; add coconut oil and a few curry leaves and remove from heat.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Pacha Tomato Avial
recipes.subtitle: Raw green tomatoes and onion cooked in a coconut-cumin masala
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Grind the grated coconut, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, cumin seeds and green chillies to a coarse paste.

In a saucepan with tomatoes and onion add the ground masala with salt and 1/4 cup of water; cook on medium heat until the vegetables are well cooked and the gravy is thick; add coconut oil and curry leaves and remove from heat.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Vellarikka Kichadi
recipes.subtitle: A cooling cucumber kichadi with yoghurt, coconut and mustard tempering
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Cut cucumber into small, thin pieces.
Grind grated coconut, cumin seeds and green chillies with a little water to a fine paste.
In a saucepan cook the cut cucumber with salt and 1/4 cup of water; add the ground masala to the cooked cucumber and cook in a low flame for 5 minutes and remove from heat.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; transfer this to the cooked cucumber.
When the cooked cucumber mixture has cooled, add yoghurt and salt if necessary and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Beetroot Kichadi
recipes.subtitle: A jewel-pink yoghurt kichadi — earthy beetroot softened by coconut and cumin
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Cut beetroot into small pieces and cook in salt and 1/2 cup of water.
Grind grated coconut, cumin seeds and green chillies with a little water to a fine paste; add the cooked beetroot to this masala and grind further to a coarse paste.
Transfer the mixture to a pan and cook on low flame for 5 minutes and remove from heat.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the mixture.
When the cooked mixture has cooled, add yoghurt and salt if necessary and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Cheera Kichadi
recipes.subtitle: Red spinach softened in yoghurt, finished with mustard and black gram
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a saucepan and sauté shallots and green chillies for about 4 minutes; add grated coconut and sauté further for 2 minutes; add chopped red spinach, 1/4 cup water and salt to this and cook on a medium flame for 5 minutes and remove from heat.

Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add cumin seeds, black gram, dry red chillies and curry leaves; sauté for a few minutes and add this mixture to the cooked red spinach.
When the cooked mixture has cooled, add yoghurt and salt if necessary and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Capsicum Kichadi
recipes.subtitle: A gentle capsicum-yoghurt kichadi — crisp but just cooked, still bright
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Cut capsicum into small pieces.

Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add the cut capsicum and cook on a low flame for 5 minutes and remove from heat (be careful not to overcook the capsicum).
When the cooked capsicum mixture has cooled, add yoghurt and salt if necessary and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Vendakka Kichadi
recipes.subtitle: Crisp-fried okra folded into a coconut-yoghurt base just before serving
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with rice. You can use bitter gourd - 3, thinly sliced circular pieces instead of okra.

steps.instruction: Cut okra into thin round pieces.
Heat oil in a pan and fry the okra; remove the fried okra to a paper napkin to drain the oil and keep aside.
Grind grated coconut, cumin seeds and green chillies with a little water to a fine paste.

Heat 2 tsp of oil in a saucepan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies, curry leaves, the ground masala and salt and cook on a medium flame for 5 minutes and remove from heat.
When the cooked mixture has cooled, add yoghurt and salt if necessary and mix well.
Add the fried okra to this mixture just before serving.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Tomato Kichadi
recipes.subtitle: A bright tomato-and-yoghurt kichadi with a coconut-cumin base
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Cut tomatoes into small pieces.
Grind grated coconut, cumin seeds and green chillies to a fine paste.
In a saucepan with the cut tomatoes add the ground masala with salt and cook for 5 minutes and remove from heat.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; transfer this to the cooked tomatoes.
When the cooked tomato mixture has cooled, add yoghurt and salt if necessary and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Cabbage Thoran
recipes.subtitle: A quick, dry stir-fry of finely chopped cabbage with coconut and curry leaves
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with rice. This template works for other vegetables — beetroot, red spinach, yam, french beans, drumstick leaves, spinach, plantain stem, or a mix (e.g. carrot-beans-cabbage).

steps.instruction: Cut cabbage into very small pieces (you may use a grater).
Grind grated coconut, cumin seeds, garlic, chilli powder, turmeric powder and curry leaves together to make a coarse paste.

Heat oil in a saucepan and add mustard seeds; when the seeds pop, add dry red chillies, split green gram and the curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add the chopped cabbage, onions and salt and mix well; cover and cook on a low flame; when the cabbage is 3/4 done, add the ground masala and mix well; cook over a low flame for 3 minutes and remove from heat.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Cheeni Amarakka Thoran
recipes.subtitle: Broad beans stir-fried with onion, coconut slices and a split green-gram tempering
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Cut the broad beans into small pieces.
Grind the grated coconut, cumin seeds and garlic to a coarse paste.

In a saucepan cook the cut beans with chilli powder, turmeric powder and thinly sliced coconut with salt and 1/4 cup of water; when the beans are 3/4 done, add the ground masala; cook for another 3 minutes and remove from heat.

Heat oil in another pan and add mustard seeds; when the seeds pop, add dry red chillies, onions, split green gram and the curry leaves and sauté until the onion turns light brown; add the cooked broad beans and sauté for another 5 minutes; add salt if necessary.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Payaru Thoran
recipes.subtitle: Green gram cooked with a coconut and cumin masala — simple, protein-rich, comforting
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash the green gram and keep aside.
Grind grated coconut, cumin, garlic, chilli powder and turmeric powder together to a coarse paste.

In a saucepan cook the green gram in 3/4 cup of water over medium heat; when 3/4 done, add salt; when the green gram is well cooked, add the ground masala and mix well; cook for a further 2 minutes till the raw smell of the masala disappears.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies, curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the thoran and mix well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Sheema Chakka Thoran
recipes.subtitle: Breadfruit cubed and stir-fried with shallots, garam masala and a split-gram tempering
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Remove the skin of the breadfruit and cut into small pieces.
Grind the grated coconut, cumin seeds and garlic to a coarse paste.

In a pan with the breadfruit add 20 shallots, coriander powder, chilli powder, turmeric powder and garam masala and salt and cook with 1 cup of water over medium heat until all the water has evaporated; add the ground masala to the cooked breadfruit and mix well; cook the mixture over a low flame for a minute.

Heat oil in another pan and add mustard seeds; when the seeds pop, add dry red chillies, 5 shallots, split green gram and the curry leaves; sauté the mixture; add the cooked breadfruit and mix well; cook further on a low flame for about 5 minutes until the breadfruit turns light brown.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Payaru Mezhukkupuratti
recipes.subtitle: French beans stir-fried with mustard, chilli and thinly sliced onion
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Cut the beans and green chillies into 1 inch long pieces.
Cook the beans and green chillies with salt in very little water for about 5 minutes.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the seeds pop, add the dry red chilly, onions and curry leaves and sauté; add the cooked beans and stir fry well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Kaya Mezhukkupuratti
recipes.subtitle: Raw plantain boiled and then stir-fried in coconut oil until glossy
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Peel the raw plantain and split lengthwise; slice into small pieces and leave it in water mixed with turmeric powder for 15 minutes.
Wash the plantains well and cook them with salt in plenty of water; when well cooked, drain the excess water and keep aside.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves; add the cooked plantain and stir fry until well done.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Payaru & Kaya Mezhukkupuratti
recipes.subtitle: Long beans and plantain stir-fried with coarsely ground shallots and chilli flakes
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with rice. You can replace long beans with ivy gourd.

steps.instruction: Peel and cut the raw plantain into 1 inch long pieces.
Soak the cut plantain with turmeric powder in water for 10 minutes.
Cut the long beans into similar sized pieces.
Cook the raw plantain and long beans with salt and water in separate pans and keep aside.
Grind shallots to a coarse paste.

Heat oil in a pan and sauté the ground shallots; add the red chilli flakes and turmeric powder and sauté until the raw smell disappears; add the cooked plantain and long beans and stir fry until well done.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Beetroot Mezhukkupuratti
recipes.subtitle: Beetroot stir-fried with tomato, onion and green chilli — deep red and lightly spiced
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: In a pan cook the beetroot with salt and 1/2 a cup of water and keep aside.

Heat oil in a pan and sauté the onions and green chillies lightly; add the chopped tomatoes, turmeric powder and chilli powder and sauté for 2 minutes; add the cooked beetroot and stir-fry until well done.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Chena Mezhukkupuratti
recipes.subtitle: Yam stewed with coriander and chilli, then fried in coconut oil until it takes on colour
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Remove the skin of the yam and cut into small pieces.

Cook the yam with chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder and salt in at least 1 cup of water over medium heat until all the water has evaporated; when the yam is well cooked remove from heat and keep aside.
Heat oil in a pan and add the cooked yam and stir fry until well done.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Koorka Mezhukkupuratti
recipes.subtitle: Chinese potato boiled soft, then sizzled with mustard and dry red chilli
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with rice. Koorka is also known as Chinese potato.

steps.instruction: Wash, clean and cut the chinese potato into small pieces.
Cook the chinese potato with salt in at least 2 cups of water; cook until water evaporates; when they are well cooked remove from heat and keep aside.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté; add the cooked chinese potato and stir fry until well done.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Vazhuthananga Mezhukkupuratti
recipes.subtitle: Eggplant and green chilli slow-cooked in coconut oil until meltingly soft
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Slice the eggplant lengthwise.
Heat oil in a pan; add eggplant, green chillies and salt and stir fry over low heat until well cooked.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Pavakka Mezhukkupuratti
recipes.subtitle: Bitter gourd softened in its own juices then stir-fried with onion and green chilli
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Split the bitter gourd lengthwise and remove the seeds; slice the bitter gourd lengthwise or horizontally.
In a pan cook the bitter gourd with salt and 1/4 cup off water until the water evaporates.
Heat oil in a pan and add the cooked bitter gourd and sauté for 5 minutes; add onions and green chillies and stir fry until well done.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Kathirikka Fry
recipes.subtitle: Brinjal rounds marinated in chilli and turmeric, then shallow-fried crisp
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash and slice the brinjal into round pieces.
Add red chilli powder, turmeric powder and salt to the sliced brinjal and keep aside for 15 minutes.
Heat oil in a pan and add small quantities of the brinjal and shallow fry the pieces.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Vendakka Mezhukkupuratti
recipes.subtitle: Okra stir-fried with onion and a simple chilli-coriander-turmeric dusting
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a pan; add the okra and salt and cook until 3/4 done.
Add onions, coriander powder, chilli powder and turmeric powder and stir fry until well done.
Add curry leaves before removing from heat.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Potato Mezhukkupuratti
recipes.subtitle: Potato cubes simmered in a shallot-pepper masala, then stir-fried in coconut oil
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash, peel and cut the potato into bite-sized cubes; leave it in water so that it does not discolor.
Grind shallots, red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder and black pepper powder to a fine paste.
In a wok, add the ground masala and salt to the cubed potatoes; cover and cook over a low flame until the potatoes are well done.
Heat oil in a frying pan; add the cooked potatoes in small quantities and stir fry well.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Potato and Carrot Mezhukkupuratti
recipes.subtitle: Potato and carrot sliced into strips and tossed with ground shallots and chilli flakes
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash, peel and cut the potatoes and carrots lengthwise (1 inch strips).
Grind the shallots to a coarse paste.

Heat oil in a pan and add the ground shallots and sauté lightly; add red chilli flakes and turmeric powder and sauté further until the raw smell disappears; add the sliced potatoes, carrots and salt; cover and cook until the vegetables are well cooked; add water to cook if necessary.
Heat oil in a pan and stir fry the cooked potatoes and carrots in small quantities until well done.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Potato Stew
recipes.subtitle: Potato, onion and green chilli gently simmered in coconut milk — appam's perfect companion
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve hot with appam or idiappam.

steps.instruction: In a saucepan, cook the potatoes, onions, ginger and green chillies with salt and water.
When the potatoes are almost cooked, add the coconut milk and bring to a boil.
Add coconut oil and curry leaves.
Serve hot with appam or idiappam.

recipes.title: Nadan Chicken Curry
recipes.subtitle: A spicy chicken curry as it is usually made in Trivandrum, Kerala – a regular classic
recipes.source: Rajkumar's kitchen — adapted and photographed, 2026
recipes.notes: A detailed home-style version with step-by-step photography. Follow each step as shown to achieve an authentic Trivandrum Nadan Chicken Curry.

steps.instruction: Roast the dry red chilies separately in a dry pan. They're done when they swell up, become shiny, and start releasing smoke — remove immediately from the pan.
steps.tip: Chilies scorch fast and burnt chili is bitter. Watch for the shine and the first wisp of smoke — that's your cue.

steps.instruction: Lower the heat and roast the coriander seeds in the same pan until fragrant and golden brown.
steps.tip: You'll smell it before you see it — coriander goes from raw to fragrant to golden in under a minute on low heat.

steps.instruction: Lower the heat further and lightly roast the whole spices (fennel, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, star anise, black pepper) for less than a minute — just enough to dry them out.
steps.tip: Whole spices need only seconds. Over-roasting makes them harsh.

steps.instruction: Remove the stems from the roasted red chillies. Grind all the roasted ingredients together to a fine powder.
Thinly slice the onions.
Finely chop the green chillies, ginger and garlic together.
Heat oil in a heavy pan and add the sliced onions.
When the onions start browning, lower heat and add the chopped green chilli, ginger and garlic. Sauté for a couple of minutes.
steps.tip: Patience here is everything. The onions need to be browned and make sure the green chili, ginger and garlic are not sauteed for too long.

steps.instruction: Add the chicken pieces and sauté on high heat until the flesh turns white.
Add salt and the ground roasted masala powder. Mix thoroughly so every piece of chicken is coated.
Add one cup of hot water to the chicken and mix well.
Lower the heat to medium, cover the pan, and cook for about 5 minutes.
Add the potato cubes (optional), cover, and cook for about 10 minutes.
steps.tip: Potatoes are traditional but optional — it adds another texture to the dish.

steps.instruction: Prepare the coconut milk by stirring 3–4 heaped tablespoons of coconut milk powder into 1 cup of hot water. Add the coconut milk to the chicken through a strainer.
steps.tip: Straining catches any lumps.

steps.instruction: Keep the pan uncovered and continue cooking until the potatoes are tender and the gravy has reduced to the right consistency. Check salt and adjust if needed. Switch off the flame.
steps.tip: "Right consistency" is a matter of taste — the consistency is usually right when you see oil start floating on top of the gravy.

steps.instruction: Prepare the ingredients for the seasoning. Slice the shallots as shown.
Heat 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small seasoning pan. When the oil is hot, add mustard seeds. When they pop completely, add the sliced shallots and brown them.
steps.tip: Wait for the mustard seeds to finish popping before adding shallots.

steps.instruction: When the shallots are brown, add the curry leaves and dry red chillies. Sauté for a few seconds.
steps.tip: Curry leaves go in last — they crackle and release their aroma in seconds.

steps.instruction: Pour the sizzling seasoning over the chicken curry. Cover the pan immediately and keep it covered for a few seconds so the aromatic steam is trapped in.
steps.tip: Trapping the seasoning steam lets the aroma settle into the curry.

steps.instruction: Transfer the chicken curry to a serving bowl.

recipes.title: Chicken Stew
recipes.subtitle: A gentle, peppery, coconut-milk chicken stew — the traditional partner for appam
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with appam. You can replace the chicken with mutton.

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a pan and add onions, ginger, garlic and green chillies; sauté well.
Add black pepper powder and garam masala, followed by the tomatoes; sauté until the oil separates.
Add the cut chicken and salt with 1 cup of water; cook over a low flame.
When the chicken is about three-quarters done, add the potatoes.
When the chicken and potatoes are cooked and the gravy is reduced to the required consistency, add the coconut milk and curry leaves; bring to a boil.
steps.tip: Don't let coconut milk boil too long — add it at the end and bring to a gentle boil to keep it from splitting.

steps.instruction: Serve with appam.

recipes.title: Chicken Purattiathu
recipes.subtitle: A dry, deeply browned chicken fry with caramelised onions — rich and restaurant-style at home
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a pan and add half of the sliced onions and green chillies; sauté for a few minutes.
Add coriander powder, red chilli powder, turmeric powder and garam masala; sauté until the raw smell disappears.
Add the chicken and salt and cook over a low flame. You may add a little water if required. Cook until the water has evaporated.
In a separate pan, heat oil and add the other half of the sliced onions; sauté until the onion turns golden brown.
steps.tip: Patience here is everything — cook the second batch of onions until deeply golden, not just translucent.

steps.instruction: Add the cooked chicken and stir-fry well for 5-10 minutes. Add curry leaves and mix well.
Serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Chicken Thoran
recipes.subtitle: Chicken cooked down dry with coconut slices, shallots and a mustard-gram tempering
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: In a saucepan with chicken, add 20 shallots, coconut slices, red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, salt and half a cup of water and cook till the chicken is done and the water has evaporated.
Grind grated coconut, cumin seeds and garlic to a coarse paste; add this ground masala to the cooked chicken and keep aside.

Heat oil in a big pan and add mustard seeds, dry red chillies, black gram, 10 shallots, curry leaves and sauté lightly; add the cooked chicken with the ground masala and stir-fry well for 10 - 15 minutes over a low flame.
Serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Chicken Kothu Paratha
recipes.subtitle: Shredded paratha tossed with spiced chicken, scrambled eggs and onion — Kerala's great one-pan supper
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Clean and cut chicken into medium to large pieces.

In a saucepan with the chicken, add black pepper powder, turmeric powder and salt and cook until chicken is well done and the water has evaporated; when the chicken has cooled, remove the flesh off the bones; cut the cooked chicken to small pieces and keep aside.
Scramble eggs and keep aside.
Chop the parathas into small pieces and keep aside.

Heat 1 tbsp oil in a deep saucepan and add the thinly sliced onions, green chillies and ginger and sauté well; add red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala and sauté further until the raw smell disappears; add the cut tomatoes and sauté until the oil separates; add the cooked chicken to the masala and sauté for 3 minutes and keep aside.

In another pan, heat 1 tbsp of oil, add finely chopped onions and sauté for 2 minutes; add scrambled eggs, paratha, cooked chicken masala, chilli sauce and soya sauce and stir fry for another 2 - 3 minutes.
Serve hot.

recipes.title: Chicken Cutlet
recipes.subtitle: Spiced chicken-and-potato patties bread-crumbed and deep-fried golden
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: You can replace the chicken with minced tuna or lamb. Cutlets coated with breadcrumbs may be stored in the freezer for use at a later date.

steps.instruction: Clean and cut chicken into medium to large pieces.

In a saucepan with the chicken, add black pepper powder, turmeric powder and salt and cook until chicken is well done and the water has evaporated; when the chicken has cooled, remove the flesh off the bones; mince the cooked chicken coarsely.
Boil the potatoes, mash them and keep aside.
Heat oil in a pan and add onions, green chillies and ginger and sauté well.
When the onions turn golden brown, add the minced chicken.
Then add the mashed potatoes and mix all the ingredients well.
Remove from the pan and allow the mixture to cool.
Make lime-sized balls of the mixture and pat them to a patty shape.
Dip the cutlets into egg whites and then coat it with breadcrumbs and keep aside.
Deep-fry a few cutlets at a time till the outer coating is golden brown.

recipes.title: Fried Chicken
recipes.subtitle: Chicken thighs spiced, breadcrumbed and deep-fried until crisp
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: The chicken coated with breadcrumbs may be stored in the freezer for use at a later date.

steps.instruction: Remove skin from chicken thighs and cut horizontal slits into them.
Grind red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, garlic, ginger and black pepper powder with a little water to a fine paste.
In a saucepan, add the ground masala and salt to the chicken and cook till the chicken is well done and the water has evaporated; keep aside.
Whisk the egg whites to an even mixture; dip the cooked chicken thighs in egg white; then coat them with breadcrumbs until completely covered.
Heat oil in a pan and deep-fry a few pieces at a time.

recipes.title: Nadan Mutton Curry
recipes.subtitle: A traditional Kerala mutton curry — roasted whole spices, onions, ginger and a final coconut-milk finish
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve hot with rice or chapatti.

steps.instruction: In a pan roast the coriander seeds, dry red chillies, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, star anise and fennel seeds.
Grind the roasted masala to a fine powder and keep aside.
Heat oil in a saucepan and add onions, green chillies and ginger and sauté well.
Add the mutton, ground masala, salt and 2 cups of water and cook over a medium flame.
When the mutton pieces are cooked (add more water if required) and the gravy is reduced to the required consistency, add the coconut milk and bring to a boil.

Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies, curry leaves and thinly sliced shallots and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the curry and mix well.
Serve hot with rice or chapatti.

recipes.title: Mutton Purattiathu
recipes.subtitle: Mutton slow-cooked in a masala, then crisped in its own fried onions — dark, deep-flavoured, festive
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a deep saucepan and add half of the sliced onions, garlic, ginger and green chillies and sauté for a few minutes; add coriander powder, red chilli powder, turmeric powder and garam masala and sauté until the raw smell disappears.
Add mutton and salt with 3 cups of water and cook over a medium flame.
Cook until the water has evaporated and the mutton is well cooked.
Heat oil in a pan and add the other half of the sliced onions; sauté until the onion turns golden brown.
Add the cooked mutton and stir-fry well for 5 - 10 minutes; add curry leaves and mix well.
Serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Mutton Thoran
recipes.subtitle: Mutton cooked down dry with coconut slices and finished with a mustard-gram tempering
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: In a saucepan with mutton, add 20 shallots, coconut slices, red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, salt and 3 cups of water and cook till the mutton is done and the water has evaporated.
Grind grated coconut, cumin seeds and garlic to a coarse paste; add this ground masala to the cooked mutton and keep aside.

Heat oil in a big pan and add mustard seeds, dry red chillies, black gram, 10 shallots, curry leaves and sauté lightly; add the cooked mutton with the ground masala and stir-fry well for 10 - 15 minutes over a low flame.
Serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Mutton Kheema Curry
recipes.subtitle: Spiced minced mutton cooked with green peas and finished with a mustard-curry-leaf tempering
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a deep sauce pan; add the chopped onion, ginger and green chilli and sauté until the onions turn golden brown; add coriander powder, red chilli powder and garam masala powder and sauté till the raw smell disappears.
Add the minced mutton, peas and salt with half a cup of water and cook on a low flame.
Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning to the kheema and mix well.
Serve hot with chapatti.

recipes.title: Beef Fry
recipes.subtitle: Pressure-cooked beef stir-fried with coconut slices until deep brown — Kerala's big-flavour showpiece
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: In a pressure cooker with the beef, add half the onion, ginger, garlic, coriander powder, red chilli powder, garam masala powder and salt; mix well, add 1/2 a cup of water.
Close the cooker and cook over a medium flame.
After the first whistle, reduce flame and cook further for 15 minutes; remove and keep aside.
Heat oil in a pan; add the rest of the onion and coconut and sauté until the onion turns light brown.
Add the cooked beef and fry on a low flame until the mixture turns dark brown.
Serve with rice or chapatti.

recipes.title: Corn Beef Dry
recipes.subtitle: Canned corn beef shredded and tossed with onion, peas and green chilli — a quick household favourite
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Remove the corn beef from the can and break it up into fine shreds.
Heat oil in a pan and add onions, ginger and green chillies and sauté well.
Add the shredded corn beef and stir-fry until it turns brown.
Add cooked green peas and mix well.
Serve with chapatti.

recipes.title: Meen Curry
recipes.subtitle: Kerala's iconic tangy, coconut-based fish curry — best the day after it's made
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Add drumstick and raw mango when making tuna curry. Tastes even better the next day as the flavours settle.

ingredients.name: Fish (mackerel, sardines or tuna)
ingredients.preparation: cleaned and cut into required-size pieces

steps.instruction: Clean and cut the fish into required-size pieces.
In a pan, roast the coriander seeds and dry red chillies well.
Grind the roasted coriander seeds and dry red chillies to a fine paste with a little water.
Add grated coconut and shallots and grind further to make a coarse paste.
In a pan, add the ground masala to the fish. Add turmeric powder, fenugreek powder, tamarind juice, green chillies, curry leaves, salt and sufficient water; cook.
steps.tip: A traditional earthen 'manchatti' pot gives this curry the best flavour.

steps.instruction: When the fish is cooked and the gravy is reduced, add coconut oil and curry leaves; mix well.
Serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Thenga Arakkatha Meen Curry
recipes.subtitle: A coconut-free red fish curry, sharp with gambooge and a hit of raw coconut oil at the end
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve hot with rice. 'Arakkatha' means ground-coconut-free — this curry is lighter and keeps longer.

ingredients.name: Fish (kingfish)
ingredients.preparation: cleaned and cut into required-size pieces

steps.instruction: Clean and cut the fish into required-size pieces.
Heat oil in a pan and add shallots, garlic and ginger; sauté well.
Add red chilli powder and turmeric powder and sauté on a low flame for a minute.
steps.tip: Keep the flame low — chilli powder burns easily and turns bitter.

steps.instruction: Add 2 cups of water, gambooge, curry leaves, fenugreek powder and salt; bring to a boil.
Add the fish pieces and continue boiling for 3-4 minutes.
Lower the heat and reduce the sauce. Once the fish is cooked, add coconut oil and curry leaves.
steps.tip: The raw coconut oil drizzled at the end is non-negotiable — it's the signature finish.

steps.instruction: Mix well and serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Pacha Thenga Aracha Meen Curry
recipes.subtitle: Anchovies or prawns cooked in a fresh, unroasted coconut and red-chilli paste
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Clean fish and keep aside.
Grind the dry red chillies to a fine paste with a little water; add grated coconut and shallots and grind further to a coarse paste.
In a pan add the ground masala to the fish; add tamarind juice, turmeric powder, fenugreek powder, curry leaves, salt and sufficient water and cook.
When the fish is cooked and gravy is reduced, add coconut oil and curry leaves.
Mix well and serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Valla Curry
recipes.subtitle: Sardines in a roasted-coriander and red-chilli gravy with tamarind and fenugreek
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Clean and cut the fish to required size pieces.
In a pan roast coriander seeds and dry red chillies well.
Grind coriander seeds and dry red chillies to a fine paste with a little water.
In a pan with the fish, add the ground masala, tamarind juice, ginger, shallots, green chillies, turmeric powder, fenugreek powder, curry leaves and salt with sufficient water and cook.
When the fish is cooked and gravy is reduced, add coconut oil and curry leaves.
Mix well and serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Vatticha Meen Curry
recipes.subtitle: A thick, slow-reduced fish curry of mackerel or sardine in coriander-coconut paste
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Clean and cut fish into required size pieces.
In a pan roast coriander seeds and dry red chillies well.
Grind coriander seeds and dry red chillies into a fine paste with a little water; add grated coconut and shallots and grind further to make a coarse paste.
In a pan add the ground masala to the fish; add turmeric powder, fenugreek powder, tamarind juice, green chillies, curry leaves, salt and sufficient water and cook.
Continue cooking on a very low flame until the gravy is very thick; add coconut oil and curry leaves and mix well.
Serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Aattu Meen Curry
recipes.subtitle: Freshwater fish simmered in a peppery coconut and curry-leaf paste
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Clean and cut the fish into required sized pieces.
Grind grated coconut, dry red chillies, peppercorns, shallots and curry leaves to a coarse paste.
In a pan add the ground masala to the fish with turmeric powder, salt and sufficient water and cook.
When the gravy is reduced to give the required consistency, add coconut oil and curry leaves.
Mix well and serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Unakka Natholi Curry
recipes.subtitle: Dried anchovies cooked with raw mango in a coconut-chilli paste — intense, sharp, delicious over rice
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Soak the dried anchovies in water for 3 - 4 hours; remove bones, clean and keep aside.
Grind grated coconut, red chilli powder, turmeric powder and shallots to a coarse paste.
In a pan with the fish add the ground masala, raw mangoes, green chillies, 1 tbsp tamarind juice, salt and sufficient water and cook on a low flame.
When the mangoes in the mix are cooked, check for sourness; if required add more tamarind juice.
When the fish is cooked and the sauce is reduced to the required consistency, add coconut oil and curry leaves.
Mix well and serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Meen Peera Pattichathu
recipes.subtitle: Sardines tossed with a very coarse coconut-ginger-garlic paste and cooked down to a fragrant, semi-dry mix
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve hot with rice. A meen chatti (clay pan) gives the best results.

steps.instruction: Clean and cut the sardines into two pieces each.
Grind grated coconut, shallots, ginger, garlic, green chillies and curry leaves to a very coarse paste.
In a pan (preferably a meen chatti) with the fish, add the ground masala, gambooge, salt and a little water.
Cook on a high flame initially and then reduce the flame and cook further until the sauce is reduced to a very thick consistency.
Serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Chemmeen Curry
recipes.subtitle: Prawns in a tangy, red-chilli-and-coconut gravy with tamarind and fenugreek
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Clean and de-vein the prawns.
Grind dry red chillies to a fine paste with a little water; add grated coconut, shallots and curry leaves and grind further to a coarse paste.
In a pan with the prawns add the ground masala, turmeric powder, fenugreek powder, tamarind juice, salt and sufficient water and cook on a medium flame.
When the fish is cooked and gravy is reduced, add coconut oil and curry leaves.
Mix well and serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Chemmeen Thoran
recipes.subtitle: Prawns cooked dry in a coconut-cumin-garlic paste with shallots and curry leaves
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Clean and de-vein the prawns.
In a pan with the prawns, add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala, shallots, salt and 1/4 cup of water and cook.
Grind grated coconut, cumin seeds and garlic to a coarse paste.
When the prawns are almost cooked, add the ground paste and mix well; continue cooking until the prawns are well cooked; remove from heat and set aside.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop add dry red chillies, black gram, a few shallots and curry leaves and sauté well.
Add the cooked prawns to the sauté and stir-fry until they turn light brown.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Chemmeen Fry
recipes.subtitle: Prawns marinated in a chilli-coriander-pepper paste and shallow-fried crisp
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash and de-vein the prawns.
Grind shallots, red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder and black pepper powder with a little water to a fine paste.
Cook the prawns with ground masala and salt.
Shallow-fry the cooked prawns until well done.

recipes.title: Chemmeen Varatharachathu
recipes.subtitle: Prawns in a dark roasted-coconut gravy, finished with a shallot-and-mustard tempering
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash and de-vein the prawns.
In a pan roast grated coconut, dry red chillies, coriander seeds and shallots until the coconut turns brown.
Grind the roasted masala with water to a fine paste.
In a pan with the prawns add the ground masala, tamarind juice, turmeric powder, salt and sufficient water and cook.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add the dry red chillies, shallots and curry leaves and sauté for a minute.
When the prawns are cooked and the gravy is reduced to the required consistency, add this seasoning.
Mix well and serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Therachi Varatharachathu
recipes.subtitle: Stingray in a dark roasted-coconut gravy sharpened with tamarind and pepper
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve hot with rice. Stingray is known as therachi.

steps.instruction: Clean and cut the stingray into 1 inch pieces.
In a pan roast grated coconut, dry red chillies, coriander seeds and shallots until the coconut turns brown.
Grind the roasted masala with water to a fine paste.
In a pan with the fish add the ground masala, tamarind juice, turmeric powder, salt and sufficient water and cook.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add the dry red chillies, shallots and curry leaves and sauté for a minute.
When the fish is cooked and water reduces to the required consistency, add this seasoning.
Mix well and serve hot with rice.

recipes.title: Meen Varathathu
recipes.subtitle: Fish fillets marinated in a chilli-pepper-ginger paste and shallow-fried until crisp on both sides
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Clean and cut fish to required size pieces.
Grind shallots, garlic, ginger, red chilli powder, turmeric powder and black pepper powder to a fine paste.
Marinate the fish with this masala and salt and keep aside for 1 hour.
Shallow-fry the fish for 10 - 15 minutes until it is properly fried on both sides.

recipes.title: Mutta Roast
recipes.subtitle: Boiled eggs smothered in a thick, onion-heavy roasted masala — the perfect appam companion
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Boil the eggs, remove the shells, and keep aside.
Heat oil in a pan; add onion, green chillies, garlic and ginger; sauté until the onions turn light brown.
Add the chopped tomatoes and sauté further until the oil separates.
Add coriander powder, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, ½ cup of water and salt; mix well.
Cook on a low flame and remove when the sauce has a very thick consistency.
steps.tip: The masala should coat a spoon — nearly dry, not saucy. That's what makes it a 'roast'.

steps.instruction: Remove from heat, add coriander leaves and the boiled eggs; mix to coat the eggs with the masala.
Serve with appam.

recipes.title: Nadan Scrambled Eggs
recipes.subtitle: A quick, peppery Kerala-style scramble with curry leaves and mustard seeds
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Crack the eggs into a mixing bowl, add salt, and whisk to make an even mixture.
Heat oil in a frying pan and add mustard seeds. When they pop, add onion, green chillies, dry red chilli and curry leaves; sauté for 2 minutes.
Add the egg mixture and cook on medium heat, folding the mixture frequently to scramble.
steps.tip: Fold gently and slowly — it's the difference between creamy curds and rubbery lumps.

steps.instruction: Serve with bread or rice.

recipes.title: Mutta Curry
recipes.subtitle: Boiled eggs simmered in a masala-rich coconut-milk gravy — pairs beautifully with appam
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Boil eggs, remove shell, and keep aside.
Heat oil in a pan; add onion, green chillies, ginger and sauté until the onions turn light brown.
Add coriander powder, red chilli powder, garam masala powder and turmeric powder and sauté until the raw smell disappears.
Add 1 cup of water and salt to the sauté and bring to a boil.
Add the boiled eggs and cook for about 3 minutes.
Add coconut milk and bring to a boil; reduce gravy to your required consistency.
Remove from heat, add coriander leaves and mix well.
Serve with Appam or Chapatti.

recipes.title: Mutta Thoran
recipes.subtitle: Scrambled eggs tossed through a coconut-chilli masala — a quick, dry side for rice
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Break eggs into a bowl, add salt and whisk to make an even mixture and keep aside.
Grind grated coconut, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, cumin seeds, garlic and curry leaves to a coarse paste.
Heat oil in a pan; add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop add black gram, dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute.
Add the ground masala and sauté further until the raw smell disappears.
Add the egg mixture to the sauté and cook over medium heat, folding continuously to scramble the eggs.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Mutta Avial
recipes.subtitle: Boiled eggs and potatoes folded into a coconut-chilli paste — Kerala's gentle egg avial
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Boil eggs, remove shell and cut into four equal wedges and keep aside.
Cook potatoes in boiling water, cool, peel and cut into wedges (to match the egg pieces).
Grind grated coconut, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, cumin seeds and garlic to a coarse paste.
Transfer the ground masala to a shallow sauce pan and add coconut oil, curry leaves and salt and mix well.
Add the boiled eggs and potatoes (do not stir); cook on medium heat until the water evaporates.
Serve with rice.

recipes.title: Ghee Rice
recipes.subtitle: Fragrant basmati perfumed with whole spices and ghee — a family feast centrepiece
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash and soak the rice for half an hour; drain and keep aside.
Heat ghee in a deep pan and add cardamom, cloves and cinnamon; sauté for a minute.
Add the chopped onions and sauté for another minute.
Add rice, mix well and sauté for 4 minutes.
steps.tip: Toasting the rice in ghee before adding water keeps the grains separate and aromatic.

steps.instruction: Add double the volume of water and salt; bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat, cover and cook over a low flame for 15 minutes, or until the water has evaporated.
Serve with chicken curry.

recipes.title: Vegetable Pilau
recipes.subtitle: An everyday one-pot rice with carrot and beans — plain enough for a weeknight, dressed enough for guests
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with chicken curry or any gravy of choice.

steps.instruction: Wash and soak the rice for half an hour; drain and keep aside.
Heat oil in a deep pan; add the onion and sauté for 3 minutes.
Add ginger and garlic paste; sauté until the raw smell disappears.
Add the vegetables and sauté for a further 3 minutes.
Add rice, mix well and sauté for 4 minutes.
Add double the volume of water and salt; bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat, cover and cook over a low flame for 15 minutes, or until the water has evaporated.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve with chicken curry.

recipes.title: Chicken Biryani
recipes.subtitle: Layers of spiced chicken and ghee-fragrant basmati rice, crowned with fried onions and coriander
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve hot with yoghurt salad and pappadom. You can replace the chicken with mutton to make mutton biryani.

steps.instruction: Heat oil in a pan and fry the thinly sliced onions and keep aside.

Heat oil in another pan and sauté the finely chopped onions till golden brown; add garlic and ginger and sauté further for 3 minutes; add red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder and garam masala and sauté till the raw smell disappears; add the chicken, tomatoes, coriander leaves and salt and cook over a low flame until the chicken is cooked and the gravy becomes thick; add a few curry leaves and half a cup of fried onions to the cooked chicken and keep aside.
Wash and soak the rice for half an hour; drain and keep aside.
