Heat ghee in a deep pan and add cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, star anise and the rice and sauté for 5 minutes; add 1 liter of water, salt and 1/2 tsp of turmeric powder and bring to a boil; cook over a low flame for about 15 minutes or until the water has evaporated completely (please ensure to not overcook the rice).

In a deep pan, add a layer of rice, then a layer of fried onions and coriander leaves; follow this with a layer of cooked chicken; repeat these layers topping up with a layer of rice and fried onions.
Serve hot with yoghurt salad and pappadom.

recipes.title: Fish Biryani
recipes.subtitle: Fried kingfish layered with a poppy-seed-and-yoghurt masala and saffron-tinged basmati — baked to meld
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve hot with yoghurt salad and pappadom. The rice is prepared the same way as Chicken Biryani.

ingredients.name: Basmati rice and whole spices
ingredients.preparation: prepared as in Chicken Biryani recipe

steps.instruction: Clean and cut fish into required size pieces.
Mix the red chilli powder, turmeric powder, salt and water to make a paste; apply this masala paste to the fish and marinate for 15 minutes; deep-fry the fish and keep aside.

In a deep saucepan, transfer the fried fish oil and add the sliced onions and sauté well; add green chillies, ginger, garlic and sauté for 2 minutes; add coriander powder and red chilli powder and sauté further; add the cut tomatoes, yoghurt, salt and ground poppy seeds; when the sauce is reduced, add the fried fish, 1 tsp garam masala, coriander leaves and lime juice and mix gently without breaking the fish; cook over a low flame for 10 - 15 minutes; when the water has evaporated, remove from heat and keep aside.
Prepare the rice as in the Chicken Biryani recipe.

In a deep saucepan, add a layer of rice followed by a sprinkling of garam masala; add a layer of fish masala; repeat these layers, topping up with a layer of rice; cover with a lid and place in a hot oven (180C) to 'dum' for 10 - 15 minutes; remove from oven and mix well.
Serve hot with yoghurt salad and pappadom.

recipes.title: Prawn Biryani
recipes.subtitle: Prawns in a coriander-chilli masala layered with spiced basmati rice and fried onions
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with yoghurt salad and pappadom. The rice is prepared the same way as Chicken Biryani.

ingredients.name: Whole spices for rice
ingredients.preparation: prepared as in Chicken Biryani recipe

steps.instruction: Wash and de-vein the prawns and keep aside.
Heat oil in a pan and fry the thinly sliced onions and keep aside.
Grind 2 onions, green chillies, garlic, ginger, tomatoes, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder and coriander leaves with a little water to a coarse paste.
Heat oil in a deep saucepan; add the ground masala and salt and sauté for 5 minutes; add the prawns and cook until the water has evaporated.
Prepare the rice as in the Chicken Biryani recipe.
In a deep pan, add a layer of rice, then a layer of fried onions and coriander leaves; followed by a layer of cooked prawns; repeat these layers topping up with a layer of rice and fried onions.
Mix well and serve with yoghurt salad and pappadom.

recipes.title: Curd Rice
recipes.subtitle: Cooked rice stirred through with milk, yoghurt and a mustard-ginger tempering — cooling, soothing, simple
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Wash rice and cook in water; drain and keep aside.
Add milk and butter to the rice when it is cooled and mix well.
Heat oil in a pan; add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add the bengal gram, chopped ginger and green chillies and sauté for 3 minutes; add curry leaves.
Add this mixture to the cooked rice; add salt and yoghurt and mix well.
Serve with pickle.

recipes.title: Bisi Bele Bath
recipes.subtitle: A deeply spiced one-pot rice-and-lentil dish from Karnataka, finished with a ghee-fried cashew tempering
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

ingredients.name: Tamarind juice
ingredients.preparation: a lime sized ball soaked in 3 cups of water

steps.instruction: Wash rice and 200 g of split pigeon peas; cook in a pressure cooker with water (the water level should be an inch above the rice); at the first whistle, remove from heat and keep aside.
In a pan roast all the masala ingredients until the grated coconut is browned; grind the roasted masala with a little water to a fine paste.

In a deep saucepan take 2 tbsp of oil, add 1 tsp mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add the bengal gram, black gram and 1 sprig of curry leaves and sauté well; add all cut vegetables and sauté for 5 minutes; add 3 cups of tamarind juice, turmeric powder and salt and cook the vegetables; when the vegetables are cooked add the ground masala and boil for 5 minutes.
Add the cooked rice and gram and salt to the vegetables, cook in a low flame for 5 minutes; remove from heat.
Heat ghee in a pan, add 1 tsp mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop add cashew nuts and 1 sprig of curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add this mixture to the Bisi Bele Bath, mix well and serve.

recipes.title: Pongal
recipes.subtitle: Rice and green gram cooked down with ghee, pepper and cumin — a creamy, comforting one-pot classic
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Roast the green gram and keep aside to cool.

Wash rice and the green gram; cook these with cumin seeds, peppercorn, chopped ginger, curry leaves and salt with water in a pressure cooker (the water level should be about 4 inches above the ingredients).
Remove from heat after the first whistle and keep aside for the steam to release.
Heat ghee in a pan, add cashew nuts and fry until they are browned; add the fried cashews to the cooked pongal, mix well and serve.

recipes.title: Chicken Noodles
recipes.subtitle: A home-style Indo-Chinese stir-fry of spaghetti, chicken, scrambled eggs and vegetables
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Cook the spaghetti in boiling salt water, al dente (follow package instructions); drain the water; add butter and the soya sauce to the spaghetti; mix well and keep aside.

Cook the chicken with black pepper powder, turmeric powder, salt and water if required until all the water has evaporated; let the chicken cool and remove the flesh off the bones and cut into tiny cubes.
Prepare scrambled eggs and keep aside.
Cook the beans and carrot in a shallow frying pan over a low flame and keep aside.
Grind garlic and black peppercorns using a pestle and mortar to a coarse paste.

Heat oil in another pan and add onion, ginger and green chillies and sauté until the onions are golden brown; add the ground garlic and peppercorns followed by crumbled chicken stock and sauté for 3 minutes; add the cooked chicken pieces and vinegar and stir fry for 2 minutes.
Remove from heat and add mixed vegetables, scrambled eggs, and chopped coriander leaves; mix well.
Transfer the mixed stir fried chicken and vegetables to the noodles and mix well.
Serve hot.

recipes.title: Pakkavada
recipes.subtitle: Crisp, spiraled rice-flour savouries pressed through a 'seva nazhi' — tea-time essentials
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve with hot tea. A 'seva nazhi' (pakkavada press/sieve) is needed to shape the dough.

steps.instruction: Mix the raw rice powder, gram flour, red chilli powder, asafetida powder, salt and butter. Gradually add water and knead to a soft dough that can be pressed through the seva nazhi.
Heat oil in a wok or kadai.
Fill the seva nazhi with the mixture and press it directly in a circular motion over the whole surface of the hot oil.
steps.tip: Press in one continuous spiral — don't lift between strokes or the pakkavada breaks into pieces.

steps.instruction: Fry the pakkavada until it turns golden brown.
Remove and drain on paper napkins.
Serve with hot tea.

recipes.title: Ulli Bhaji
recipes.subtitle: Crisp onion fritters in a spiced gram-flour batter — the rain-and-chai snack
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Mix gram flour, red chilli powder, asafetida and salt. Add water gradually to make a batter of a consistency that will evenly coat the sliced onions.
Heat coconut oil in a deep wok (kadai).
Dunk the onion slices into the batter to coat them evenly.
Slide the coated onion pieces with a spoon into the hot oil and deep-fry until golden brown.
steps.tip: Coconut oil is worth it — regular oil works, but the flavour won't be the same.

steps.instruction: Remove and drain on paper napkins.
Serve with hot tea.

recipes.title: Murukku
recipes.subtitle: Crisp, spiral rice-flour snacks pressed fresh and fried until golden — Kerala's teatime favourite
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

ingredients.name: Black gram
ingredients.preparation: fried and ground into a powder

steps.instruction: Mix the raw rice, black gram, til seeds, butter and salt; gradually add water to this mixture and knead to make a soft dough to press through the 'seva nazhi' (murukku presser/sieve).
Heat oil in a wok or kadai.
Fill the seva nazhi with the mixture and press it onto the hot oil in the pan in a circular motion to make a three inch spiral; repeat according to the size of your wok.
Fry the murukku until the colour changes to golden brown.
Remove and drain on paper napkins.
Serve with hot tea.

recipes.title: Thairuvada
recipes.subtitle: Soft black-gram vadas steeped in a cooling coconut-yoghurt, garnished with coriander and chilli
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Garnish with coriander leaves and red chilli powder before serving.

steps.instruction: Whisk yoghurt with water to get the required consistency.
Grind the grated coconut, ginger and green chillies with water to a fine paste; add the ground mixture and salt to the yoghurt and mix well.

Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds; when the mustard seeds pop, add dry red chillies and curry leaves and sauté for a minute; add this seasoning and roasted cumin powder to the yoghurt and mix well.
Wash and soak the black gram in plenty of water for 6 hours; drain and keep aside.
Grind the black gram with a little water to make a fluffy batter; transfer the batter into a bowl, add salt and mix well.

Heat oil in a deep wok (kadai); with a wet palm take a ball sized batter and drop into the hot oil; repeat based on the size of your pan; deep-fry until they turn crisp and golden brown; remove and drain the vadas on paper napkins.
Transfer the vadas to the yoghurt mixture and mix.
Garnish with coriander leaves and red chilli powder before serving.

recipes.title: Ethakka Appam
recipes.subtitle: Ripe plantain slices dipped in a turmeric-touched batter and deep-fried — sweet, crisp, golden
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Cut the ripe plantains into two halves each horizontally and then slice the halves vertically into three thin slices.
Mix all purpose flour, rice flour, turmeric powder and salt; add water gradually to make a batter of a consistency to evenly coat the sliced bananas.
Heat coconut oil in a deep wok (kadai).
Dunk the banana slices into the batter to coat them evenly.
Slide the coated banana pieces into the hot oil and deep fry until golden brown.
Remove and drain on paper napkins.
Serve with hot tea.

recipes.title: Unniappam
recipes.subtitle: Small jaggery-sweetened rice dumplings fried in a special pan — temple-prasadam made at home
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Uses a special 'unniappa thattu' (dimpled cast-iron pan).

steps.instruction: Soak rice in water for 3 hours; drain water until the rice is dry; grind the rice to a coarse powder.
Add jaggery to 1/4 cup of water and heat in a low flame until it dissolves; strain the liquid to remove the impurities.
Add the dissolved jaggery to the ground rice powder, mix well and keep the batter aside for 3 hours.
Grind the ripe bananas to a fine paste; add the banana paste and coconut slices to the batter just before frying and mix well.
In an unniappa thattu, pour oil; once the oil is hot pour the batter and fry until the unniappam is cooked on both sides, turning once.

recipes.title: Semiya Payasam
recipes.subtitle: A warm, milky vermicelli pudding fragrant with cardamom and studded with ghee-fried cashews
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Crush the vermicelli into small pieces and keep aside.
Add water to the milk powder to make thick milk.

In a big saucepan, heat 1 tbsp of ghee and fry the vermicelli for 5 minutes; add water and boil over a low flame; continue cooking until the vermicelli is well cooked (add more water if necessary); add sugar and stir for five minutes in a low flame; add the thick milk and bring to a boil; add cardamom powder and fresh milk and bring to a boil again and remove from heat.
In a shallow pan heat 1 tbsp of ghee and fry the cashew nuts and raisins; sprinkle this over the payasam.

recipes.title: Parippu Payasam
recipes.subtitle: Green gram cooked in jaggery and coconut milk, finished with sago and ghee-fried nuts — the Onam sadhya sweet
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: You can replace the green gram with bengal gram to make Kadala Pradhaman.

steps.instruction: Cook sago in water and keep aside.
Add jaggery to 1/4 cup of water and heat in a low flame until it dissolves; strain the liquid to remove the impurities.
Wash the green gram, add sufficient water and cook; drain the water and keep the cooked green gram aside.
In a saucepan, heat ghee and add the cooked green gram and dissolved jaggery.

Cook on a low flame, stirring continuously until the ghee separates and the mixture acquires a thick consistency; add thin coconut milk and the cooked sago to this mixture and bring to a boil; add cardamom powder and the thick coconut milk and bring to a boil again and remove from heat.
In a shallow pan heat 1 tbsp of ghee and fry the cashew nuts, coconut slices and raisins; sprinkle this over the payasam.

recipes.title: Ari Payasam
recipes.subtitle: A rich rice and jaggery pudding with mashed banana, grated coconut and cardamom — served warm
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Slit the bananas vertically and cut into small pieces.
Add jaggery to 1/4 cup of water and heat in a low flame until it dissolves; strain the liquid to remove the impurities.

Wash rice, transfer to a saucepan and fill with water to cover the rice; cook rice well until most of the water has evaporated; add the dissolved jaggery to the cooked rice, mix well and continue cooking for about 5 minutes; add 1 tbsp of ghee, the chopped bananas, grated coconut and cardamom powder and mix well; cook until a semi-thick consistency is achieved.
In a shallow pan, heat 1 tbsp of ghee and roast the cashew nuts and raisins; sprinkle this mixture over the payasam and serve warm.

recipes.title: Ada Pradhaman
recipes.subtitle: Rice-flake ada in a thick jaggery and coconut-milk reduction — the crown of an Onam feast
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection
recipes.notes: Serve warm. The showpiece sweet of the Onam sadhya.

steps.instruction: Cook sago in water and keep aside.
Add jaggery to 1/4 cup of water and heat in a low flame until it dissolves; strain the liquid to remove the impurities.
Fill a big saucepan with plenty of water and boil; add the ada to the boiling water and cook well; when the ada is well cooked drain excess water and wash with water.
In a thick-based saucepan (uruli), heat ghee; add the washed ada and dissolved jaggery.

Cook on a low flame, stirring continuously until the ghee separates and the mixture acquires a very thick consistency; add thin coconut milk and the cooked sago to this mixture and bring to a boil; add cardamom powder and the thick coconut milk and bring to a boil again and remove from heat.
In a shallow pan, heat ghee and fry the thinly sliced coconut, raisins and cashew nuts; sprinkle over the pradhaman and serve warm.

recipes.title: Plain Cake
recipes.subtitle: A classic vanilla butter sponge — the humble tea-time cake baked a thousand times over
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Remove butter from refrigerator and defrost overnight in room temperature.

Beat eggs and caster sugar together in a food processor for about ten minutes at a medium speed; add butter and blend again for three minutes; add self-raising flour and blend again for a further minute; transfer the mixture to a bowl and add vanilla essence and mix well.
Preheat the oven to 180 C.

Grease the baking tray with butter and pour the cake mix; bake the cake in the oven for about 40 - 45 minutes; when the top of the cake appears baked, pierce the cake with a skewer to check if it is baked through.

recipes.title: Date Cake
recipes.subtitle: A moist sponge soaked with overnight-plumped dates and studded with cashew — deeply fragrant, gently sweet
recipes.source: Good food at home — A Kerala Family Recipe Collection

steps.instruction: Deseed and chop the dry dates into small pieces.
Add bicarbonate of soda to water and mix well; soak the dates in this mixture overnight.

Beat eggs and caster sugar together in a food processor for about ten minutes at a medium speed; add butter and blend again for three minutes; add self-raising flour and blend again for a further minute; transfer the mixture to a bowl and add vanilla essence, mix well and keep aside.
Blend the soaked dates in the food processor for about three minutes; remove and add the date mixture and chopped cashew nuts to the above mixture and mix well.
Preheat the oven to 180 C.

Grease the baking tray with butter and pour the cake mix; bake the cake in the oven for about 40 - 45 minutes; when the top of the cake appears baked, pierce the cake with a skewer to check if it is baked through.
